Name: spectral_latency_optimization
Title: Spectral Latency Optimization for Training-Free Machine Learning
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that spectral stability bounds and PAC-Bayes frameworks can be leveraged to develop training-free machine learning methods for latency-critical inference, enabling efficient and reliable deployment in resource-constrained environments.
Related Work: Our proposal builds upon recent works in spectral quantization routing, geometric signal separation, and training-free geometric interference cancellation. However, our approach differs in its focus on spectral latency optimization and the integration of PAC-Bayes frameworks for rigorous uncertainty quantification and latency guarantees.
Abstract: We propose a novel training-free machine learning framework that leverages spectral stability bounds and PAC-Bayes frameworks to optimize latency-critical inference. Our approach, termed Spectral Latency Optimization (SLO), dynamically allocates numerical precision and computes spectral diagnostics on-the-fly to enforce strict latency budgets. We theoretically ground SLO in information-theoretic bounds, linking spectral conditioning to generalization stability under quantization noise. Evaluated on single-GPU setups across diverse benchmarks, SLO achieves a superior throughput-accuracy Pareto frontier compared to static and heuristic mixed-precision baselines.
Experiments: Evaluate SLO on WILDS benchmarks for natural distribution shifts
Compare SLO with static and heuristic mixed-precision baselines on single-GPU setups
Assess the impact of spectral conditioning on generalization stability under quantization noise
Investigate the effectiveness of SLO in enforcing strict latency budgets and reducing hidden-state perturbation overhead
Risk Factors and Limitations: The complexity of spectral computations may impact real-time performance
The choice of spectral diagnostics and precision-switching policy may require careful tuning
The applicability of SLO to diverse machine learning tasks and datasets requires further investigation
